name: Video Games
description: Level up your knowledge of gaming history and culture.

questions.text: What is the name of the plumber protagonist in Nintendo's most famous franchise?
questions.explanation: Mario is Nintendo's iconic plumber and the main protagonist of the Super Mario franchise, first appearing in Donkey Kong (1981).

questions.text: Which company created the Sonic the Hedgehog franchise?
questions.explanation: Sega created Sonic the Hedgehog in 1991 as a mascot to compete with Nintendo's Mario, developed by Yuji Naka and his team.

questions.text: What year was the original PlayStation released in Japan?
questions.explanation: The original PlayStation was released in Japan on December 3, 1994, and in North America in September 1995.

questions.text: Which game features the character Link and the Triforce?
questions.explanation: The Legend of Zelda franchise features Link as the main protagonist and the Triforce as a central plot element, created by Nintendo's Shigeru Miyamoto.

questions.text: What is the best-selling video game of all time?
questions.explanation: Minecraft is the best-selling video game of all time, with over 300 million copies sold across all platforms as of 2024.

questions.text: Which console introduced the concept of motion controls to mainstream gaming?
questions.explanation: The Nintendo Wii, released in 2006, pioneered mainstream motion controls with its Wiimote, making gaming accessible to broader audiences.

questions.text: In which game do players survive on an island by building structures and fighting monsters?
questions.explanation: Minecraft allows players to mine resources, build structures, and survive against monsters in an open-world sandbox environment.

questions.text: What franchise features the character Master Chief?
questions.explanation: Master Chief (John-117) is the protagonist of the Halo franchise, created by Bungie and now developed by 343 Industries for Microsoft.

questions.text: Which gaming company is known for the Pokemon franchise?
questions.explanation: Pokemon was created by Game Freak and is published by Nintendo and The Pokemon Company, with the franchise beginning in 1996 in Japan.

questions.text: What does 'NPC' stand for in gaming?
questions.explanation: NPC stands for Non-Playable Character, referring to characters in a game that are controlled by the game's AI rather than by a player.

questions.text: Which game popularized the battle royale genre with its 'PUBG' abbreviation?
questions.explanation: PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds (PUBG), released in 2017 by Brendan Greene, is credited with popularizing the battle royale genre.

questions.text: What is the name of the antagonist in the original Donkey Kong arcade game?
questions.explanation: In the original Donkey Kong arcade game (1981), Donkey Kong is the antagonist who kidnaps Pauline while Mario (Jumpman) must rescue her.

questions.text: Which franchise is set in the post-apocalyptic Commonwealth and features the Pip-Boy?
questions.explanation: The Fallout franchise features the iconic Pip-Boy wrist computer and is set in a retro-futuristic post-nuclear apocalypse America.

questions.text: In Street Fighter II, which character shouts 'Hadouken'?
questions.explanation: Ryu is the main character of the Street Fighter series and is known for his Hadouken (fireball) and Shoryuken (dragon punch) moves.

questions.text: What was the first commercially successful home video game console?
questions.explanation: The Atari 2600 (1977) was the first commercially successful home video game console, popularizing the concept of interchangeable cartridges.

questions.text: Which game developer created the Dark Souls series?
questions.explanation: FromSoftware, directed by Hidetaka Miyazaki, created the Dark Souls series, known for its challenging gameplay and intricate lore.

questions.text: What does 'FPS' stand for in gaming?
questions.explanation: FPS stands for First-Person Shooter, a genre of video game where the player experiences the action through the protagonist's eyes.

questions.text: Which game series features the phrase 'It's dangerous to go alone! Take this.'?
questions.explanation: This famous line is from the original The Legend of Zelda (1986) for NES, spoken by an old man who gives Link his first sword.

questions.text: What year did the first Grand Theft Auto game release?
questions.explanation: The original Grand Theft Auto was released in 1997 by DMA Design (later Rockstar North) for MS-DOS and Windows.

questions.text: Which company developed the Unreal Engine game engine?
questions.explanation: Unreal Engine was developed by Epic Games, first released in 1998 with Unreal, and has become one of the most widely used game engines.

questions.text: What is the highest possible score in the classic arcade game Pac-Man?
questions.explanation: The maximum possible score in Pac-Man is 3,333,360 points, achieved by eating every dot, energizer, ghost, and fruit on all 256 levels.

questions.text: Which game was the first to feature online multiplayer through Xbox Live at launch?
questions.explanation: MechAssault was one of the launch titles for Xbox Live in November 2002 and was the first game to feature Xbox Live online multiplayer.

questions.text: In which game do players manage a theme park, keeping visitors happy while turning a profit?
questions.explanation: RollerCoaster Tycoon (1999) by Chris Sawyer is a theme park management game where players design, build, and manage an amusement park.

questions.text: What is the name of the esports tournament for Dota 2, known for its massive prize pools?
questions.explanation: The International (TI) is Dota 2's annual championship, famous for its enormous crowd-funded prize pools, reaching over $40 million in 2021.

questions.text: Which game popularized the 'Metroidvania' sub-genre along with Castlevania: Symphony of the Night?
questions.explanation: Super Metroid (1994) on SNES, along with Castlevania: Symphony of the Night, defined the 'Metroidvania' genre of non-linear exploration platformers.

questions.text: What is the name of the fictional country in Metal Gear Solid called Outer Heaven?
questions.explanation: Zanzibar Land is the fictional militarized nation in Metal Gear 2: Solid Snake, serving as the stronghold of Big Boss in that game.

questions.text: Which studio developed the original BioShock?
questions.explanation: BioShock (2007) was developed by Irrational Games, led by Ken Levine, and is set in the underwater dystopia of Rapture.

questions.text: In World of Warcraft, what is the name of the human capital city?
questions.explanation: Stormwind City is the capital city of the human kingdom in World of Warcraft and the main hub for Alliance players.

questions.text: Which game holds the record for the most concurrent players on Steam?
questions.explanation: Dota 2 holds the all-time peak concurrent player record on Steam, reaching over 1.29 million simultaneous players in March 2016.

questions.text: What was the name of Nintendo's game console that failed commercially and preceded the Nintendo 64?
questions.explanation: The Virtual Boy (1995) was Nintendo's failed stereoscopic 3D game console that displayed only red graphics and caused eye strain, discontinued within one year.

questions.text: Who is the main character in the Devil May Cry series?
questions.explanation: Dante, a demon hunter who is the son of the demon knight Sparda, is the main protagonist of the Devil May Cry series.

questions.text: Which game pioneered the 'looter-shooter' genre with its procedurally generated weapons?
questions.explanation: Borderlands (2009) pioneered the looter-shooter genre by combining FPS gameplay with procedurally generated weapons and RPG loot systems.

questions.text: In Final Fantasy VII, what is the name of the company that controls the world's energy?
questions.explanation: Shin-Ra Electric Power Company is the mega-corporation that controls the world in Final Fantasy VII, draining the planet's life energy (Mako).

questions.text: What is the name of Lara Croft's debut game?
questions.explanation: Lara Croft debuted in the original Tomb Raider (1996), developed by Core Design and published by Eidos Interactive.

questions.text: Which game developer created the God of War franchise?
questions.explanation: God of War was created by Santa Monica Studio, an internal Sony studio, with the franchise beginning in 2005 directed by David Jaffe.

questions.text: What was the first video game to be inducted into the World Video Game Hall of Fame?
questions.explanation: Pong was among the six inaugural inductees into the World Video Game Hall of Fame in 2015, recognized for its foundational role in gaming.

questions.text: What is the term for the technique in speedrunning where players manipulate the game's random number generator?
questions.explanation: RNG manipulation involves exploiting the random number generator's deterministic nature to control in-game randomness for favorable outcomes.

questions.text: Who founded id Software, the studio behind Doom and Quake?
questions.explanation: id Software was co-founded by John Romero and John Carmack (along with Tom Hall and Adrian Carmack) in 1991 in Dallas, Texas.

questions.text: What is the name of the algorithm used in most video game pathfinding?
questions.explanation: The A* (A-Star) algorithm is the most widely used pathfinding algorithm in video games due to its efficiency and accuracy in finding shortest paths.

questions.text: What is the longest continuous gaming session world record holder's approximate time?
questions.explanation: The Guinness World Record for longest gaming marathon is approximately 138 hours and 34 seconds, set by Carrie Swidecki playing Just Dance in 2015.

questions.text: Which game used the SCUMM engine, pioneering point-and-click adventure games at LucasArts?
questions.explanation: Maniac Mansion (1987) was the first game built using the SCUMM (Script Creation Utility for Maniac Mansion) engine, developed by LucasArts.

questions.text: What was the internal code name for the Nintendo Switch during its development?
questions.explanation: The Nintendo Switch was developed under the codename 'NX' before its official reveal and release in March 2017.

questions.text: In competitive Street Fighter, what is the term for a move that hits opponents even while they are blocking, reducing their guard meter?
questions.explanation: Chip damage (block damage) is damage dealt to a player who is blocking; in Street Fighter V, players can be KO'd by chip damage from special moves.

questions.text: What programming language was used to originally develop the game Minecraft?
questions.explanation: Minecraft was originally developed by Markus 'Notch' Persson in Java, which is why the PC version is often called 'Java Edition.'

questions.text: Which game's modding community created the original Battle Royale concept that led to PUBG?
questions.explanation: Brendan Greene created the Battle Royale mod for Arma II (a DayZ variant) before developing the standalone game that became PUBG.

questions.text: What is the name of the technique in fighting games where a player inputs a reversal move on the exact first frame of waking up?
questions.explanation: A reversal is when a player successfully inputs a move on the first available frame (frame 1) when recovering, often used to escape pressure situations.

questions.text: What is the highest tier rank in competitive League of Legends?
questions.explanation: Challenger is the highest tier in League of Legends ranked play, limited to the top 300 players per server/region.

questions.text: Which game was the first to use ray tracing technology in real-time rendering on consumer hardware?
questions.explanation: Battlefield V was the first major game to launch with real-time ray tracing support on Nvidia RTX consumer graphics cards in November 2018.

questions.text: What is the term for the technique in 3D games where developers hide loading between areas using forced slow animations?
questions.explanation: The crawl tunnel technique (used in God of War, The Last of Us) uses forced slow crawling animations to mask background level loading without loading screens.

questions.text: Which gaming studio holds the Guinness World Record for the most critically acclaimed studio based on Metacritic scores?
questions.explanation: Valve holds the Guinness World Record for the most critically acclaimed game developer, with an average Metacritic score of 96.15 across its titles.
